name: Morehead Community
description_short: Morehead State University is a public regional university in Kentucky offering undergraduate and graduate programs, not a consumer finance company or payday alternative lender.
description_long: Morehead State University (MSU) is a public regional university located in Morehead, Kentucky, founded as an educational institution. MSU is ranked #12 among public regional universities in the South by U.S. News & World Report and has been elevated to Carnegie Classification as a Research College and University for 2025. The university is recognized for programs in space science, medical careers, engineering, education, agriculture, business, and STEM fields. MSU offers 65 undergraduate majors, dozens of master's and doctoral programs, and graduate certificates across multiple campus locations (Morehead, Ashland, Mt. Sterling, Prestonsburg) and online/hybrid formats. The institution awarded $48.6 million in scholarships and grants last year from federal, state, institutional, and external sources. Students benefit from a 16:1 student-to-faculty ratio and 98% of students receive financial aid. The university operates the Level UP! program, which provides career-oriented courses, faculty mentoring, and hands-on experience to over 3,500 students. MSU distinguishes itself through significant campus investment—nearly $300 million in revitalization projects including new science and engineering buildings and residence halls. The university emphasizes research opportunities, internships, service learning, and education abroad programs. MSU is investing in student success through initiatives like the Level UP! program and maintains partnerships including NASA Space Grant recognition. The institution also operates Morehead State Public Radio and actively engages in community and business partnerships. This company listing is fundamentally miscategorized. Morehead State University is an accredited educational institution, not a consumer finance company, lender, or payday alternative provider. It has no relationship to credit products, emergency loans, debt relief, or any financial services category. The categorization as 'payday-alternatives' appears to be a data entry error. The correct classification should be removed from consumer finance databases entirely, as universities are educational institutions outside the scope of consumer lending or financial services comparison platforms.
